questions: Should single-clicking a shot group label ONLY select the clips, or should it also visually highlight the shot group (e.g., with a border/glow)? The current double-click just calls selectClips — should we add additional visual feedback?
When the agent auto-resolves shot membership for create_task, should it silently attach to the shot or should it mention the shot in its response text (e.g., 'Adding to shot: Hero shots')?

For the 'place after selected' workflow, should the agent compute placement relative to the END of the last selected clip, or relative to the end of the entire shot group? (e.g., if 3 clips are selected from a 5-clip shot, place after clip 3 or after clip 5?)

The current selected_clips payload is sent from the frontend on each message. Should shot_id/shot_name be resolved on the frontend (from pinnedShotGroups) and sent in the payload, or should the edge function resolve it server-side from the database? Frontend resolution is simpler but may miss shots that aren't pinned to the timeline.

success_criteria.criterion: Single-clicking a shot group label in the timeline selects all clips in that group (verified by selectedClipIds containing all group clip IDs)
The timeline summary sent to the agent includes a 'Shot groups:' section listing pinned shot groups with their names, clip IDs, and modes
Each clip line in the timeline summary includes a 'shot=<name>' tag when the clip belongs to a pinned shot group
Selected clips sent to the agent include shot_id and shot_name when the clip belongs to a shot
When all selected clips belong to the same shot, create_task auto-populates shot_name if not explicitly provided
All existing tests in tool-calls.test.ts continue to pass
New tool-calls.test.ts tests cover structured tool_calls extraction, run() pattern, numbered lists, and isToolError()
New timeline.test.ts tests cover addMediaClip, duplicateClip, describeTimeline with shot groups, and formatClipLine with shot tags
New workflow.test.ts tests verify the duplicate/place-after-selected multi-step sequence produces correct timeline config
Right-click on shot group label still opens context menu
duplicate_generation attaches the new generation to the same shot as the source generation
Selected clip prompt in the agent system prompt includes at= and duration= from timeline context
New test files follow Deno.test pattern consistent with existing tool-calls.test.ts
Manual smoke test: open agent chat with a shot group selected, verify agent response references the shot by name

assumptions: Single-click on shot group label replaces the current context-menu-on-click behavior; context menu moves to right-click only (which already works).

Shot group metadata in the timeline summary uses the existing pinnedShotGroups from TimelineConfig, not a separate DB query — this means only pinned (visible on timeline) shots appear in the agent context.
The duplicate/place workflow tests will use Deno.test with the existing test runner pattern from tool-calls.test.ts.
The agent's system prompt already documents the duplicate/place workflow (prompts.ts:154-158) and doesn't need text changes — only the underlying data enrichment and tests.
SelectedClipPayload's shot_id and shot_name fields are optional to maintain backwards compatibility with clips that don't belong to any shot.

The edge function will resolve shot context server-side (via the existing enrichClipsWithPrompts pattern) rather than relying solely on frontend-sent shot_id, ensuring correctness even for gallery selections that aren't pinned.
